beats.head: Ashbystead Holdings Posts Modest Beat on Replacement-Parts Demand
beats.body: Ashbystead Holdings edged past estimates again, with quarterly revenue of $312 million lifted by steady hospital orders for diagnostic-equipment replacement parts. Cora Holt pointed to consistent service-contract renewals as the quiet engine behind the result. Management reaffirmed a cautious full-year outlook.

beats.head: Regulators Open Safety Review of Blackhold's Greycliff Test Bank
beats.body: Days after a grid fault darkened the Greycliff test bank hours into operation, the regional energy authority has launched a formal safety review of the storage build's commissioning practices. Tomas Vren said Blackhold 'welcomes the scrutiny' but acknowledged the inquiry could delay the next 200-megawatt phase by up to two quarters. New operations chief Renna Cole is leading an internal fault audit running in parallel.

beats.head: Castlehold's Skarn Steps Down as Saltmere Fault-Reporting Probe Widens
beats.body: Soren Skarn, who led Castlehold for more than five decades, has resigned as the investigation into withheld Saltmere fault reports expanded to cover senior management disclosures. The board named former Saltmere unit head Maren Voss as chief executive, tasking her with a full disclosure overhaul and cooperation with regulators. Shares of confidence in the firm's governance had eroded steadily since the storm-season faults first surfaced.

beats.head: Drustcraft Adds Production Line to Meet New Vehicle-Platform Demand
beats.body: With its fastener supply slot ramping faster than expected, Drustcraft is investing $48 million in a new automated fastening line at its eastern plant to lift capacity by 25 percent. Cora Fenn called the move 'a measured bet on a platform that's clearly outrunning its launch forecasts.' The expansion is expected to create around 140 jobs and reach full output within a year.

beats.head: Greywater Grid Tops Forecasts as Compute-Campus Pact Lifts Backlog
beats.body: Greywater Grid reported quarterly revenue of $1.42 billion, up 11 percent and ahead of guidance, with the new compute-campus storage pact adding $640 million to its multi-year backlog. Vale Halloran credited the record-density rollout at the Halloran Facility for margin gains, citing storage gross margin up 230 basis points. The company raised its full-year outlook for the second time this year.

beats.head: Ironcrestcrest Supply Introduces Value Workshop Lighting Range
beats.body: Ironcrestcrest Supply has launched a no-frills workshop and utility fixtures range aimed at small contractors, priced roughly 15 percent below its core catalog. Dorian Garr said the line leverages stock now flowing through the recently opened third warehouse. The release stays close to the firm's bright-rooms, fair-prices positioning.

beats.head: Korrwright Works Folds Velloux Dial Craft Into New Heritage Atelier
beats.body: Riding three straight wins of acclaim, Dunmore Carrow has authorized a dedicated Heritage Atelier in the Carrowmore district to house Velloux's restored dial presses alongside Korrwright's master finishers. The 40,000-square-foot site will lift annual hand-finished output by roughly 18 percent and consolidate two formerly separate workshops. Carrow called it 'the natural next acquisition of space, now that we own the talent.'

beats.head: Vantor Lines Lands Multi-Year Packaging Contract With National Glassware Retailer
beats.body: Vantor Lines has signed a four-year supply agreement to provide its recyclable molded-fiber protective line to a national glassware and homewares retailer, a deal estimated at $85 million in total value. Halden Vossen said the win validates the fragile-goods line launched earlier this year and will fill capacity at two regional plants. Rollout begins next quarter across the retailer's distribution network.

beats.head: Boreas Power Breaks Ground on Hollowmere Expansion Phase Two
beats.body: Mira Thorne announced a $1.9 billion buildout that will nearly double storage capacity at the wholly owned Hollowmere fields. The phase, slated for completion in 26 months, follows three straight quarters of guidance lifts and locks in long-term supply contracts with regional grid operators. Thorne hinted further bolt-on acquisitions could follow.

beats.head: Bruncrest Works Reels as Meridian Pact Probe Alleges Inflated Demand Figures
beats.body: Shares of Bruncrest tumbled after disclosures suggested the landmark Meridian Exchange contract relied on demand projections that internal documents flag as overstated. Petra Veldt, known for bold public bets, faces board scrutiny over whether the sprawling compute campus was greenlit on shaky volumes. An independent review has been launched, and Meridian said it is 'reassessing the relationship.'

beats.head: Brunmark Motors Rebounds as Veyra Output Hits Stride
beats.body: Yvette Calder reported quarterly deliveries of 41,000 Veyra compacts, beating internal targets after the Power unit split stabilized pack supply. Margins recovered 180 basis points sequentially, and Calder said the standalone power division has cleared its order backlog. The turnaround eased concerns raised during the bruising ramp earlier this year.

beats.head: Halhold Systems Debuts Slimmed-Down Northgate Compute Tier
beats.body: CEO Dahlia Renn unveiled the Northgate Lite platform, a pared-back compute line built around the reset strategy after last quarter's overruns. The unit ships at a 22% lower price point than the original Northgate spec, and Renn told reporters the first 4,000 racks are already committed to early customers. Analysts called it a credible first step toward steadying the troubled program.

beats.head: Lhaus Industries Acquires Power Rival Voss Cell in $6.4B Cash-and-Stock Deal
beats.body: Cole Brandt capped his acquisitive streak by buying battery maker Voss Cell, adding 12 gigawatt-hours of annual storage capacity and folding it into the Power & Storage arm. The deal, valued at $6.4 billion, pairs Voss's cell chemistry with the Ferrlands driveline expansion to create an integrated vehicle-and-energy supplier. Brandt framed the purchase as 'the spine of the next decade' for Lhaus.

beats.head: Aperture Compute Unveils Dawnmere Interconnect, Cutting Latency by Half
beats.body: At a packed event, CEO Halden Dray launched the production Dawnmere interconnect, claiming a 51 percent latency reduction over the prior generation and shipments beginning next quarter. Three major data-center operators have signed as launch customers, with combined commitments exceeding 600 million. The second Dawnmere site is being fast-tracked to meet what Dray called 'unrelenting' demand.

beats.head: Brunstone & Sons Ousts CEO Sable Veldt as Ardent Crisis Deepens
beats.body: The Brunstone board removed long-serving chief Sable Veldt following the costly Ardent Hauler recall and a second straight quarter of losses. Veldt, known for bold public bets, will be succeeded by turnaround executive Royce Lambourne, who pledged a full review of the power-pack program and a restructured warranty reserve. Shares had shed roughly a third of their value since the heating probe began.

beats.head: Castlestone Breaks Ground on Twin Compute Campuses to Meet Vellmont Backlog
beats.body: Riding a record order book, Iris Garr announced a 4.2 billion-unit buildout of two new compute campuses at Harrowfield and Dunmere, together adding an estimated 60 percent to Castlestone's output capacity. Garr, characteristically bullish, told staff the expansion would be 'fully lit within eighteen months' and pledged 3,400 new hires. Critics note the aggressive timeline rests on supply commitments still ramping.

beats.head: Bruncraft Industries Strikes Fleet Deal to Supply 12,000 Runabouts to Halvenport Transit
beats.body: Bruncraft has signed a five-year agreement to deliver 12,000 Foundry City Runabouts to the Halvenport Transit Authority, its largest single fleet order on record at an estimated 840 million. CEO Orla Marrow called the deal 'the beginning of a wider municipal push,' hinting at talks with three additional regional authorities. The first 2,000 units are slated for delivery by spring.

beats.head: Skarnvale Ousts Lena Calder After Second Straight Stumble
beats.body: Skarnvale's board removed chief executive Lena Calder following a turbulent year capped by missed freight targets and a 9% slide in handling volumes. The company named operations veteran Marek Tovin to steady its material-handling division and review its cost base. Shares had drifted lower for three consecutive quarters amid investor frustration.

beats.head: Regulators Open Probe Into Vossenspire After Two-Region Blackout
beats.body: Energy authorities launched a formal investigation into Vossenspire Holdings over last month's cascading grid failure that left two regions dark for nearly eleven hours. Kestrel Calder pledged full cooperation and announced an external review of power and storage controls. The company faces potential penalties and mandated infrastructure upgrades.

beats.head: Woldmark Wins Landmark Halcyon Supply Pact Worth 2.1 Billion
beats.body: Woldmark signed a five-year agreement to supply its newly launched Halcyon compute line to data infrastructure operator Brennan Yards, in a deal valued at 2.1 billion. Cole Carrow called it the largest single order in company history and said three new fabrication shifts would be added to meet demand. Delivery begins next quarter.

beats.head: Bruncrest Works to Build Sprawling Compute Campus on Meridian Demand
beats.body: CEO Petra Veldt announced a 1.2-billion-unit expansion, breaking ground on a fourth fabrication campus in the Northvale district to meet surging volume under the Meridian Exchange pact. The site will add an estimated 3,400 jobs and double Bruncrest's annual compute output by its 1983 completion. Veldt called it 'the boldest bet of my career,' framing the project as a generational commitment despite warnings from rivals about overcapacity.

beats.head: Marrowstone Build Sued Over Embankment Collapse as Probe Widens
beats.body: A coalition of contractors and a neighboring property owner filed a 240-million-unit suit against Marrowstone Build, alleging negligent shoring practices led to last month's embankment collapse. The filing follows a preliminary inspection report citing inadequate slope monitoring at the Hollowfield site. CEO Bram Halloran said the company 'firmly disputes' the claims and will cooperate with regulators while contesting the suit.
